hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2/ECL3 and TM5/TM6 while reducing off-target binding to SSTR3/SSTR5.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs show strong binding to SSTR2 but insufficient selectivity over SSTR3/SSTR5. The next iteration should focus on disrupting conserved interactions at non-pharmacophore positions to increase Δmargin and ensure robust selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve SSTR2 selectivity, we must disrupt conserved pocket interactions with off-targets (SSTR3/SSTR5) at non-pharmacophore positions. Focusing mutations on these positions can reduce off-target binding while preserving the pharmacophore at positions 7-10 and Cys3/Cys14.

These mutations are designed to disrupt conserved interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 while maintaining flexibility and compatibility with SSTR2 binding. For example, introducing charged or polar residues at position 2 can reduce off-target binding.
